title: ECTS credit transfer
question: Can our students receive ECTS credits transferable to their home university?
short_answer: Yes, with caveats
details: Summer programs award ECTS credits proportional to duration + assessment intensity. Standard 2-week programs typically map to 3-6 ECTS; long-form 4-6 week tracks (Global Entrepreneurship Program) up to 12 ECTS. Credits are listed on an official H-FARM College transcript issued ~6 weeks after program end.
applies_to_label: All assessed summer programs (not orientation-only formats like Out of the Box)
constraints: Receiving university's registrar must pre-approve credit equivalency before student enrols — H-FARM College cannot guarantee acceptance, only issue the source transcript. Some universities require a sample syllabus + assessment rubric in advance.
ask_partner: Does your registrar have a standard process for recognising non-EU summer-program credits? What documentation do they typically need from the host institution?

title: Joint certificate (co-issued with partner)
question: Can the program certificate carry both H-FARM College and our university's branding?
short_answer: Yes (per-partnership negotiation)
details: Standard programs issue a solo H-FARM College certificate. Joint co-issuance requires a Memorandum of Understanding that defines co-branding terms, signatory authority, and template approval. Once the MoU is in place, every cohort from the partner gets the dual-logo version automatically.
applies_to_label: All programs once MoU is signed
constraints: Adds 4-8 weeks to the first partnership cycle (MoU review on both sides). Partner's legal must approve our certificate template; we must approve theirs. Recommended for partners committing to 2+ cohorts/year.
ask_partner: Do you require dual-branded certificates for your students' transcripts, or is a third-party institutional certificate acceptable in your system?

title: Faculty observer slots
question: Can one of our faculty members accompany the cohort and observe / co-assess?
short_answer: Yes — typically welcome and unblocks credit transfer
details: Partner-university faculty can join as observers (sitting in on sessions, attending company visits) or as co-assessors (signing off on grades alongside H-FARM College instructors). The co-assessor route significantly streamlines credit recognition at the partner's home institution because they witnessed the work firsthand.
applies_to_label: All programs (long-form benefits most)
constraints: Travel + accommodation typically partner-funded. Co-assessor role requires alignment on rubric in advance (~2 weeks pre-program). Observer slots cap at 2 faculty per cohort to keep dynamics intact.
ask_partner: Would your faculty accompanying the cohort help with your internal credit-recognition process? If so, who would attend and in what role — observer or co-assessor?

title: Transcript vs certificate format
question: What documentation do students receive — transcript with grades, or just a certificate of participation?
short_answer: Both available, on request
details: Default deliverable is a certificate of participation (issued at the closing ceremony). Programs with formal assessment additionally issue a transcript with letter grade + ECTS allocation, available ~6 weeks after program end. Partner can request transcript-only, certificate-only, or both per cohort.
applies_to_label: Assessed programs (orientation programs only issue certificate)
constraints: Letter-grade transcript requires formal assessment — students MUST complete the capstone / final project. A no-show on final assessment converts the transcript to participation-only.
ask_partner: For credit transfer, does your registrar need both the certificate AND the graded transcript, or just one?

title: Co-assessment with partner faculty
question: Can our faculty co-grade the students' final deliverables alongside H-FARM College instructors?
short_answer: Yes — recommended for credit-bearing partnerships
details: Co-assessment means the partner-university faculty member (acting as co-instructor) signs the final assessment alongside the H-FARM College lead. This dual-signature transcript carries stronger weight at the home institution and dramatically reduces friction during credit transfer review.
applies_to_label: All assessed programs
constraints: Faculty must attend the assessment-week sessions (last 2-3 days of the program). Rubric alignment workshop required 2 weeks before program start. Honorarium typically partner-funded; H-FARM College does not pay external co-assessors.
ask_partner: Would having your faculty co-sign the assessment transcript help streamline credit recognition at your end?

title: Duration adjustments (stretch / compress)
question: Can the program be shortened to 1 week or extended to 3+ weeks for our calendar?
short_answer: Limited flex — minimum 1 week, maximum 6 weeks
details: Standard durations are documented (1-2 wk, 4-6 wk). 1-week compression is possible for some programs by dropping the project-finalisation phase but the credit allocation drops accordingly. Extensions beyond 6 weeks generally require splitting into two cohort blocks rather than one continuous program.
applies_to_label: All summer programs (varies by program)
constraints: Duration changes affect ECTS allocation linearly. Faculty scheduling locks 8 weeks before program — late-stage duration changes may not be accommodatable. Custom durations may carry a 10-15% logistics surcharge.
ask_partner: What's your academic calendar's window for this — and is the ECTS / credit allocation tied to a specific duration?

title: Cohort size flexibility
question: Our group is smaller / larger than 15-30 — can you accommodate?
short_answer: Yes — 10 minimum, 50 maximum per cohort
details: Below 10 students the cost-per-student becomes uneconomical (logistics fixed costs don't scale down). Above 30 the program splits into parallel tracks with rotating instructors. Above 50 we recommend splitting into two cohort weeks rather than one mega-cohort.
applies_to_label: All summer programs
constraints: Small cohorts (<15) may incur a per-student premium to cover fixed logistics. Large cohorts (>30) require committing 8 weeks in advance to allow instructor scheduling.
ask_partner: How many students do you typically send per cohort, and is this fixed or flexible year to year?

title: Hybrid components (online + on-campus split)
question: Can some sessions be delivered online so students don't need to travel for the full duration?
short_answer: Yes — already implemented in 'Start Online · Finish On Campus' format
details: The 'Start Online · Finish On Campus' format is purpose-built for this: 1 month online (asynchronous + live workshops) followed by 1 month on-campus for company projects + final pitch. Other programs can be customised with online pre-work or post-program follow-up but core delivery stays on-campus.
applies_to_label: Start Online · Finish On Campus (native); Global Entrepreneurship (with adaptation)
constraints: Fully online versions are NOT offered — H-FARM College's differentiation is the immersive campus + company-visit experience. Pure online would erode that. Hybrid with substantial on-campus block is the floor.
ask_partner: What's the visa / travel constraint that's driving the hybrid request — would a shorter on-campus block (e.g. 1 wk on-campus + 1 wk online) work for your students?

title: Single-cohort scheduling (isolated for one partner)
question: Can our students run as their own cohort, not mixed with students from other universities?
short_answer: Yes, with minimum group size
details: Single-partner cohorts run when the group hits 15+ students (full cohort minimum). Below 15, students join a mixed cohort for cost reasons. Single-cohort delivery enables tailored content, language flex, and concentrated peer-bonding which most partner unis value highly.
applies_to_label: All summer programs (15+ student threshold)
constraints: Below 15 students, partner can either pay a buyout premium for solo cohort OR accept mixed-cohort placement. Custom content for solo cohorts requires 6-week pre-program lead time.
ask_partner: Would your students benefit from cohort isolation (e.g. delivery in your language, focus on your industry sector) or are they comfortable in a mixed international cohort?

title: Pre-program campus visit (partner staff)
question: Can our staff visit the campus before sending students, for compliance / safety inspection?
short_answer: Yes — welcome and free
details: H-FARM College hosts partner-university staff for half-day or full-day site visits prior to cohort arrival. Covers: housing facilities, classroom infrastructure, safety procedures, food / dietary, accessibility, emergency contacts. Often paired with introductions to instructors and a tour of company partners.
applies_to_label: Not program-specific — applies before any cohort delivery
constraints: Partner-funded travel + accommodation. Best scheduled 4-8 weeks before cohort arrival. Multiple staff possible; we ask for advance notice for facility tours.
ask_partner: Does your institution require an in-person site inspection before approving student travel? If so, which areas (safety / academic / housing) are the priority?

title: Group discounts (15+ students)
question: Do you offer pricing breaks for larger cohorts?
short_answer: Yes — tiered discount structure
details: Tiered group discount typically applies: 15-20 students baseline; 21-30 receive ~10% off per-student fee; 31+ receive ~15% off. Exact structure negotiated per partnership; multi-year commitments unlock additional ~5%.
applies_to_label: All summer programs (cohort-based pricing)
constraints: Group discount applies to the per-student fee only — not to logistics / housing / company-visit costs (those scale with headcount). Multi-year discount requires signed MoU not just intent.
ask_partner: How many students are you considering, and is this a one-time or recurring arrangement? Multi-year commitments unlock better pricing.

title: Scholarships for high-performers
question: Do you offer merit scholarships for outstanding students from our university?
short_answer: Yes — limited slots per partnership
details: Per-partnership merit scholarships available — typically 1-2 partial scholarships (30-50% off) per cohort of 15+ students. Selection is joint: partner shortlists top candidates by GPA + statement of purpose, H-FARM College's academic council reviews and confirms.
applies_to_label: All summer programs (long-form preferred — more impact per scholarship)
constraints: Scholarship eligibility requires partner-university nomination + demonstrated financial need OR exceptional merit. Not available retroactively; must be in MoU. Cannot stack with group discount on the same student.
ask_partner: Would access to merit scholarships strengthen your students' ability to apply? What's the typical financial constraint you encounter?

title: Tuition waivers for partner faculty
question: Can our faculty members access H-FARM College executive education or master's programs at reduced cost?
short_answer: Yes — partnership perk, capped per year
details: Strategic partnerships can include 1-2 tuition waivers per year for partner-university faculty wanting to attend H-FARM College Executive Education modules or short-form Master's electives. Builds long-term collaboration goodwill at low marginal cost for us.
applies_to_label: Not for summer programs — applies to faculty development tracks
constraints: Strategic-partner-only perk (signed MoU, 2+ cohorts/year). Waivers cover tuition only, not housing or travel. Not available for full-degree master's enrolment.
ask_partner: Would your faculty be interested in attending H-FARM College executive education as part of the partnership? Which areas (innovation, AI, design thinking) are most relevant to them?

title: Performance-based renewals (pilot → multi-year)
question: Can we start with a single pilot cohort and lock in multi-year pricing if it goes well?
short_answer: Yes — preferred structure for new partnerships
details: Recommended for first-time partners: signed MoU covers a Year-1 pilot. If post-cohort survey shows ≥80% student satisfaction AND the partner confirms intent to send a second cohort, the MoU auto-renews into a 2-year locked pricing structure. Removes the 'big commitment up front' barrier.
applies_to_label: All collaboration formats; structural rather than program-specific
constraints: Pilot pricing slightly higher than locked-in multi-year (the discount kicks in on renewal). Auto-renewal requires both the satisfaction threshold AND explicit partner confirmation — no automatic billing.
ask_partner: Would a low-commitment pilot-first structure help you get internal approval faster? What success criteria would you want defined upfront?

title: Topic focus shift (per-cohort customisation)
question: Can the curriculum lean toward a specific industry or topic relevant to our students?
short_answer: Yes — moderate flex within each program's core theme
details: Each program has a core theme (e.g. Fashion AI Lab → AI applications in fashion / retail) but case studies, company visits, and capstone projects can shift toward a partner's preferred industry vertical. Example: 'AI in Fashion' can lean 'AI in Logistics' if partner students come from supply-chain background — keeps the AI core, swaps the application layer.
applies_to_label: Programs with project-based learning (orientation programs less flex)
constraints: Major shifts (e.g. 'Game Design' → 'Logistics') aren't possible — those are different programs. Requires 6-week pre-program planning for company-visit alignment.
ask_partner: What industry or topic focus would resonate most with your students' background and post-program career intent?

title: Mother-tongue support (TA / orientation)
question: Can you provide a teaching assistant who speaks our students' language for orientation and Q&A?
short_answer: Yes — for cohorts of 15+ in a single language
details: Optional native-language teaching assistant for non-English-native cohorts. TA handles orientation (logistics, expectations, cultural adjustment), runs weekly Q&A in native language, and serves as translation backup during company visits. Especially valuable for Turkish, Spanish, Chinese, and Korean cohorts.
applies_to_label: All summer programs (cohort 15+ in single language)
constraints: Requires 15+ students in same language to be cost-effective. TA is supplementary — core instruction remains in English. Cannot replace English-language assessment requirements.
ask_partner: What is your students' typical English level (CEFR), and would a native-language TA make a meaningful difference for their experience and outcomes?

title: Industry partner alignment (company visits)
question: Can company visits feature firms relevant to our region or sector?
short_answer: Partial flex — within H-FARM Group's network
details: Company visits draw from H-FARM Group's portfolio (200+ startups + corporate partners). When a partner-university cohort has clear sector focus, we curate visits accordingly — e.g. Turkish cohort interested in logistics gets visits to logistics-tech startups + multinational logistics company HQs in Veneto.
applies_to_label: All programs with company-visit components
constraints: Limited to firms in our network. Cannot guarantee specific named companies (their availability fluctuates). Sector match is targeted but exact partner-region match (e.g. 'Turkish companies') is generally NOT possible since visits are Italy-based.
ask_partner: Which industries or company types would create the strongest learning + network value for your students post-program?

title: Capstone project IP ownership
question: Who owns the intellectual property from student capstone projects?
short_answer: Default: student retains, with non-exclusive licence to H-FARM College + sponsor
details: Default IP arrangement: student retains ownership of capstone work; H-FARM College and (if applicable) the sponsor company receive a non-exclusive royalty-free licence for educational + portfolio use. This balances student incentive with our right to showcase work. Custom arrangements possible per MoU (e.g. sponsor-exclusive for projects with commercial application).
applies_to_label: Programs with project-based / capstone deliverables
constraints: Custom IP terms (e.g. partner-uni ownership claim) must be in MoU before cohort starts — cannot be applied retroactively. Sponsor-company projects sometimes carry NDA; students sign individually before joining sponsored streams.
ask_partner: Does your institution have a standard IP policy for student work produced during external programs? Are there specific requirements we should align with?

title: Reverse mobility (H-FARM College → partner exchange)
question: Can your students come to us as well as ours coming to you — true bidirectional exchange?
short_answer: Yes — most established partnerships eventually become bidirectional
details: Reverse mobility means H-FARM College bachelor / master students attend the partner's summer programs or semester exchanges, balancing the student flow. Common structure: Year 1 unidirectional (partner sends to us), Year 2 begins reciprocal flow once mutual program-fit is confirmed. Strengthens partnership beyond transactional 'they send us students' dynamic.
applies_to_label: Structural arrangement, not program-specific
constraints: Requires partner to offer programs / semester slots that fit H-FARM College academic calendar and degree-progression rules. Volume rarely matches one-for-one — typically asymmetric (e.g. 20 of theirs to us, 5 of ours to them) and that's fine.
ask_partner: Would your institution be interested in receiving H-FARM College students for short programs or semester exchanges? What would the natural fit be on your side?

title: Co-branded marketing & joint recruitment
question: Can we co-promote the partnership through joint marketing materials and recruitment campaigns?
short_answer: Yes — encouraged once MoU is signed
details: Signed-partnership perks include: dual-logo recruitment flyers, joint social media announcements, co-hosted information sessions (in-person or webinar), shared press releases on first cohort. H-FARM College marketing team provides design support; partner approves all material before publication.
applies_to_label: Activates after MoU signature
constraints: Brand-use guidelines apply on both sides — partner uni logo cannot appear on H-FARM College material without explicit per-asset approval. Social media co-posts coordinated; no unilateral announcements before MoU is publicly signed.
ask_partner: What co-marketing assets would create the most value at your end — recruitment flyers, info sessions, alumni testimonials, social announcements? Who on your side leads marketing approvals?
